title: Java Lazy Constants
description: Initialize a value lazily and at most once with LazyConstant.
learningGoals: Defer expensive initialization with a LazyConstant that is computed at most once.
Note: lazy constants are a preview feature in Java 26, enabled with --enable-preview.

choices: The supplier runs every time get() is called
The field can be reassigned after initialization
The value is initialized before it is returned
completionChecks.explanation: LazyConstant.get() initializes the content if needed, then returns the initialized value.

completionChecks.prompt: When is System.getLogger("app") called in this lesson?
choices: When the containing object is loaded, before any get() call
Only when logger.get() first needs the value
completionChecks.explanation: The supplier passed to LazyConstant.of is lazy. It runs on the first get() that needs the value and then the initialized value is reused.

completionChecks.prompt: Which trap does LazyConstant avoid compared with an eager static field?
choices: It avoids doing expensive initialization before the value is needed
It makes the value mutable after every call
It turns System.Logger into a stream collector
completionChecks.explanation: Lazy constants defer creation until the value is read. The API is still preview here, but the point is delayed one-time initialization.
